comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review advisory triage (all 8 valid, resolved in impl/test design): (1)+(2) verify-over-exact-bytes: dsse.py exposes pae(payload_type,body)->bytes + envelope encode/decode; byte-identity test asserts decoded payload == original bytes and pae() runs over those exact bytes, not json.dumps(json.loads(...)). (3) DSSE v1.0.0 §3: envelope payload & sig are base64url-encoded; decode base64url-decodes before PAE — impl + test. (4) file_impact now set. (5)+(6) envelope encode/decode round-trip + fixed-vector decode tests added. (7) error-case edge tests: empty payload_type, malformed/missing-field envelope JSON. (8) create src/rebar/attest/__init__.py for the namespace. Re-running review-plan to re-sign after file_impact change.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
Foundational encoder for every rebar signature: the DSSE v1.0.0 envelope and Pre-Authentication Encoding so signatures cover exact opaque bytes and never a re-serialized JSON form. Everything else in the foundation builds on this.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/attest/dsse.py` implements `pae(payload_type, body)->bytes` per spec ("DSSEv1" SP len(type) SP type SP len(body) SP body) plus envelope `{payload,payloadType,signatures:[{keyid,sig}]}` encode/decode.
- [ ] Unit test verifies PAE against the DSSE spec's worked example (byte-exact).
- [ ] Unit test with duplicate/re-ordered JSON keys shows verify operates on the exact stored bytes (no re-serialization).

file_impact.reason: PAE spec-vector, byte-identity, envelope round-trip, error-case tests

compiled_state.title: attest: DSSE envelope + PAE encoder/decoder
